original_abstract: The narrative of the Good Samaritan as written in the Gospel of Luke is a familiar narrative among Christians. Some researchers have raised this narrative in terms of ethnic differences, but researchers are interested in examining this narrative from another point of view, namely religious differences. The dispute between the Jews and the Samaritans implied in this narrative is not only due to ethnic differences but also to religious differences. One of the religious conflicts that has occurred before is the imposition of Judaism on the Samaritans that occurred in Hyrcanus' time. Using a descriptive analysis approach, the researcher came to the conclusion that the narrative contains human values in terms of equality, compassion, and totality.
humanized_abstract: The Good Samaritan Story in Luke The Good Samaritan story in Luke has been known to Christians for centuries. Researchers have addressed this text using various approaches especially focusing on ethnicity, but this time it is attempted to explore it through religious criteria by taking into account the religious conflict between Jews and Samaritans. The ethnic conflict behind this text can be supported and proved by historic facts, especially when Judaism was imposed on Samaritans during Hyrcanus time. The researcher will deal with this story using the descriptive analysis approach and proving that human values such as equality, mercy and totality are contained in this story.

The researcher will deal with this story using the descriptive analysis approach and proving that human values such as equality, mercy and totality are contained in this story.
